review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they address the management of service accounts, which can be considered analogous to guest accounts in this context. The naming conventions mostly follow the required pattern, but a slight adjustment was made for clarity. The functions map well to the RBAC controls in Kubernetes, ensuring least privilege and auditing are enforced.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'disabled' instead of 'no' for clarity in 'k8s_rbac_no_guest_service_accounts'
title: Guest Account Management
step1_reasoning: The control focuses on managing guest accounts, which in Kubernetes can be mapped to service accounts that should not have unnecessary permissions. Ensuring that there are no service accounts with excessive privileges or that are not properly audited aligns with the intent of managing guest accounts. Kubernetes RBAC can be used to enforce least privilege and audit service account usage.
